content: When scaffolding a new module, check docker-compose.*.yml and PLATFORM-SERVICE-CATALOG.md for port allocations before choosing service and DB ports
Always create a dedicated integration proof bead at phase boundaries to verify cross-module contracts before starting the next phase
State machine entities must emit an event on every transition (not just creation) — downstream consumers need the full lifecycle for auditing and reactive processing
When adding FK-like columns (receipt_id, part_id) to a table, always create indexes for the query patterns those columns will serve
Keep Docker/CI wiring in a separate bead from domain logic — infrastructure port conflicts and config issues should not pollute domain commits

New module scaffolds follow a standard 18-file template: Cargo.toml, Dockerfile.workspace, migration, config, db/resolver, domain/outbox, events/mod, http/health, http/tenant, lib, main, metrics — copy from the most recent scaffold
Integration proof tests should cover regression of existing behavior — e.g. purchase receipt tests must still pass after adding production receipt support
Disposition and inspection event payloads must include both the previous and new state values so consumers can determine the transition direction without querying the source
